name: Southern Bancorp Community Partners
description_short: Non-profit CDFI offering free HUD-certified credit and housing counseling, financial coaching, and lending to underserved communities across Arkansas and Mississippi.
description_long:
Southern Bancorp Community Partners (SBCP) is a non-profit Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) and certified affiliate of Southern Bancorp, Inc., based in Little Rock, Arkansas. The organization focuses on building wealth and opportunity in underserved communities through financial services and education. Founded with a mission to serve low-to-moderate income individuals and communities, SBCP operates as a HUD-certified housing and credit counseling agency.

SBCP offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including HUD-certified housing counseling, financial coaching, credit counseling, free tax preparation through their Volunteer Income Tax Assistance program, and financial education through their Learning Center. They also provide development lending for business loans, consumer loans, and asset-building programs. The organization maintains a Financial Fitness Blog covering topics like credit utilization and financial wellness. Recent initiatives include a down payment assistance program providing up to $12,000 for minority and low-to-moderate income homebuyers announced in January 2024.

What distinguishes SBCP is their dual mission as both a counseling agency and lender, allowing them to address financial challenges from multiple angles. Their HUD certification and non-profit status mean counseling services are genuinely free and driven by client benefit rather than profit. They operate in underserved rural and urban areas (Arkansas and Mississippi), specifically targeting demographics often excluded from traditional financial services. Their public policy advocacy work and America Saves Pledge program demonstrate commitment beyond individual transactions.

The primary caveat is geographic limitation—services are currently available only in Arkansas and Mississippi. While their counseling is free and high-quality, their lending products (business and consumer loans) are separate services that involve credit decisions and terms not detailed on the website. Consumers should verify current eligibility and service availability before applying.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling provided by trained professionals at no cost
Free financial coaching covering credit restoration, establishment, and building
Non-profit CDFI structure means services prioritize consumer benefit over profit
Comprehensive services in one organization—counseling, lending, and tax prep
Down payment assistance program offering up to $12,000 for eligible homebuyers
Free tax preparation through VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) program
Educational resources including Financial Fitness Blog and Learning Center
cons: Services limited to Arkansas and Mississippi only—no national reach
Website lacks specific details about loan terms, rates, and approval criteria for lending products
No information provided about counselor availability, appointment wait times, or response time for inquiries
Limited transparency on eligibility requirements for various counseling services
No mention of virtual vs. in-person counseling options beyond housing counselor virtual appointments
best_for: Low-to-moderate income homebuyers in Arkansas/Mississippi seeking down payment assistance and pre-purchase counseling
Individuals with poor or no credit history needing free credit restoration and financial coaching
First-time homebuyers requiring HUD-certified housing counseling before applying for mortgages
Low-income tax filers who qualify for free tax preparation services
diagnosis: Southern Bancorp Community Partners is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families in Arkansas or Mississippi seeking free, non-profit credit and housing counseling backed by HUD certification. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—services are not available outside these two states, and lending products are separate from counseling services with their own approval processes.
services: Credit counseling and credit building services
Financial Fitness Blog and educational content
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
